message:
fix(ui): make shorten_label use display_width instead of chars().count()

The shorten_label function was using .chars().count() for length checks
and .chars().take(n) for truncation — both count Unicode scalar values,
not terminal columns. This means CJK characters (2 columns wide) and
emoji would cause labels to overflow their column budget.

Fixed to use display_width() for length comparisons and per-character
UnicodeWidthChar::width() for truncation, consistent with the
display_width-based measurement that was introduced for label_w
calculation in 76d86714.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
